other_observers: Larry Modesitt , Ira Sanders , Joe Roller , Joyce Takemin , Maggie Boswell , Jeannie Mitchell et al.; Larry Modesitt found this bird on 11/30/2011 .
specific_location: Front Yard of 847 N 13 th St .
description: Unique plump shape of this shorebird with long straight bill indicates that this was of the Scolopax genus , and the buffy orange ( not barred ) underparts that this was an American Woodcock not Eurasian. Head had three broad black transverse bands on crown ,dark centers with white edging to the upper scapulars , very short tail upper surfaces lightly barred, buffy orange underparts make this an easy call.Behaviors: Spent most of the time resting on ground sleeping. Did get up once and walked a few inches , and did do a bobbing and rocking motion as it moved forward .Call: No calls heardPlumage: Adult
photographer: Photographer: Jeannie Mitchell, John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond
